transcript.clean_text: after yesterday's summit finished there will be no letting up for the riders today Stage three is the longest of the twenty20 Jayco herald-sun Torah 178 kilometres and includes three categorized climbs including Tawonga gap which comes straight out of the gate the final climb of terminate gap comes 16 kilometres before the finish so it should be a finally poised battle between stage hunting climbers and the sprinters hoping to hang on to the finish at Wangaratta 88 riders made the start this morning in bright and who else but Ben Hill could make the first attack as soon as the flag was dropped the bridge lane riders set the tone for the first hour which was an all-out smash fest up to one gig app and even after that 8 kilometer climb it's still no break away it established by the time we got over the top and we already had several riders off the back including Rob power of some web who is certainly no slouch on the climbs the bunch was strung out all the way to rose white the second climb of the day but by the top finally we saw a break have been established led over the line by James or am a black spoke seven other riders with him in the move ahead of a Sun Webb led Pettit on that were visibly much calmer now that the break had gone the pace was easy enough for Brendan David's to take one of the most casual bike changes you've ever seen plenty of time for him to rejoin the bunch we did have some passengers in this break with Michael rice of ära Pro Cycling refusing to do a single turn on the front all day and this prompted some attacks to try and get rid of the dead weight eight riders soon became six with Oram also dropped from the group and the lead to the bunch rose back up over four minutes with 70 kilometers to go Sun Webb were an ever-present at the head of the peloton fully in control of this breakaway as they began to close the gap the residual fatigue starting to show itself in front sites of Ebola was dropped quickly followed a couple of hundred metres later by Ben Hill although it took a monumental bout of cramp to stop the 30 year old from continuing coming to a complete halt on the side of the road to try and stretch it out he will have been disappointed not to make it to the last intermediate sprint of the day taken by Whelan who pocketed three bonus seconds in the process the tempo in the bunch was high on the final climb and the break was in touching distance as they went over the top with 16 kilometers to go stage one winner Alberto died nazy though was just off the back of the pack over the top and he had work to do to try and get back into contention for the wind today for the reindeer at the breakaway under control it was mitchelton Scott who took to the front for their sprinter Kaden grows some we're sitting back just a little to allow tiny T to rejoin past wardens to the final 1500 meters and despite a few kamikaze moves on the running we were pretty much assured of a bunch sprint yet Pro Cycling a time they're trained well to lead into the final left hander Hoffman kicked first off his teammates will with a hundred and fifty meters to go Mikhail Romm is looking lively for Israel startup nation but coming around in the lime green helmet his Cayden grows not sure what he was looking over his left shoulder for but by the time he had turned back he'd crossed the line to take his first victory in the colors of mitchelton Scott died nazy was on the Australians wheel but he couldn't come round his form a teammate of the line that's one win apiece for these two young sprinters this week here's your top ten with Rime rounding out the podium hofland in fourth and michael rice making all those skip turns in the breakaway count with fifth place on the day Jay Hindley had a relatively trouble-free day to keep hold the leader's yellow jersey it's almost as you were in the general classification with the exception of Weiland taking back three seconds in time bonuses and Rob power opting to work for Hindley today and sacrificing his overall chances in the process finishing 8 minutes and 27 seconds down in the finish and dropping out of the top ten entirely join us tomorrow for what will be another fascinating day for the GC with a summit finish up Mount Buller as always we'll have the live stream and the highlights here on TCN racing you
